tasks.title: Implement reify-syntax parse() with Tree-sitter
tasks.description: Implement the Tree-sitter grammar for the Reify M1 language subset and the parse() function that produces a ParsedModule.
tasks.details: Completed. Tree-sitter grammar and parser implemented.
tasks.testStrategy: All 8 reify-syntax tests pass.

tasks.title: Implement reify-kernel-occt OCCT FFI binding
tasks.description: Implement the OpenCASCADE FFI binding that implements the GeometryKernel trait for BRep operations needed by M1.
tasks.details:
## Context
The GeometryKernel trait is defined in reify-types/src/geometry.rs. It requires: execute() for geometry operations, export() for STEP/STL, query() for measurements, tessellate() for mesh generation. The reify-kernel-occt crate is currently a stub.

The MockGeometryKernel in reify-test-support/src/mocks.rs shows the expected API surface.

## What to implement
1. Set up OCCT dependency:
   - Use the opencascade-sys or opencascade crate, OR
   - Set up cxx bridge with a thin C++ wrapper layer
   - Add build.rs to handle C++ compilation and OCCT linking
2. Implement OcctKernel struct implementing GeometryKernel trait:
   - Primitive creation: Box, Cylinder, Sphere (via BRepPrimAPI_Make*)
   - Boolean operations: Union, Difference, Intersection (via BRepAlgoAPI_Fuse/Cut/Common)
   - Modifications: Fillet (via BRepFilletAPI_MakeFillet)
   - Transforms: Translate, Rotate
   - STEP export (via STEPControl_Writer)
   - Tessellation (via BRepMesh_IncrementalMesh)
   - Queries: Volume, Area, Centroid, BoundingBox (via GProp_GProps)
3. Handle OCCT exceptions (Standard_Failure) → Rust Result
4. Manage TopoDS_Shape lifecycle safely across FFI boundary

## Interface tests that must pass
- crates/reify-eval/tests/boundary4_geometry.rs: all 9 currently ignored occt_tests (create_box_export_step, box_volume_10mm, boolean_difference, cylinder_volume_query, fillet_edges, tessellation_valid_mesh, translate_centroid, zero_dimension_error, invalid_reference_error)

## Dependencies
- OpenCASCADE must be available as a system library or built from source
- Consider using the 'opencascade' or 'opencascade-sys' crate from crates.io if mature enough
- If not available, set up appropriate build infrastructure
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-kernel-occt and cargo test -p reify-eval -- occt_tests — all 9 OCCT-specific tests must pass.

tasks.title: Implement reify-compiler compile() function
tasks.description: Implement the compile() function that takes a ParsedModule from reify-syntax and produces a CompiledModule with TopologyTemplates.
tasks.details: Already implemented in prior merges. All interface tests pass.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-compiler — all boundary tests including currently ignored ones must pass. Compile the bracket parsed module and verify equivalence with bracket_compiled_module() fixture.

tasks.title: Implement reify-geometry DispatchPlanner
tasks.description: Implement the geometry dispatch layer that routes GeometryOp sequences to registered GeometryKernel implementations.
tasks.details:
## Context
The GeometryKernel trait and all geometry types (GeometryOp, GeometryHandle, ExportFormat, Mesh, etc.) are defined in reify-types/src/geometry.rs. The reify-geometry crate is currently a stub.

## What to implement
1. Implement DispatchPlanner struct:
   - Holds a list of registered GeometryKernel implementations
   - For M1, just routes everything to the single registered kernel (OCCT)
   - execute(): delegates operation sequence to the appropriate kernel
   - export(): delegates to kernel's export()
   - tessellate(): delegates to kernel's tessellate()
   - query(): delegates to kernel's query()
2. Implement a GeometryEngine facade (optional but useful):
   - Manages GeometryHandle lifecycle
   - Maps handle IDs to kernel-specific state
   - Provides a clean API for the eval engine to call
3. Handle the case where no suitable kernel is registered for an operation

## M1 scope
- Single kernel dispatch (no multi-kernel routing logic needed yet)
- No tolerance budget tracking
- No @optimized hook support
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-geometry — the dispatch planner correctly routes operations to a mock kernel and to the OCCT kernel when available.

tasks.title: Implement reify-eval Engine (eval/check/build)
tasks.description: Implement the Engine facade in reify-eval that orchestrates the full pipeline: parse → compile → evaluate → check constraints → build geometry.
tasks.details: Already implemented in prior merges. All interface tests pass.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-eval — all boundary tests including currently ignored ones must pass. The full pipeline with mocks and with real constraint checker must work end-to-end.

tasks.title: Implement reify-cli check and build commands
tasks.description: Implement the CLI binary with 'reify check' and 'reify build' commands that drive the full pipeline.
tasks.details: Already implemented in prior merges. All interface tests pass.
tasks.testStrategy: Build the CLI binary. Run `cargo run -p reify-cli -- check examples/bracket.ri` and verify it exits 0 with all constraints satisfied. Run `cargo run -p reify-cli -- build examples/bracket.ri -o /tmp/bracket.step` and verify it produces a file.

tasks.title: Wire up end-to-end STEP export: bracket.ri → reify build → bracket.step
tasks.description: The M1 exit criterion is `reify build examples/bracket.ri -o bracket.step` producing a valid STEP file. All individual components pass their boundary tests (parser, compiler, eval, OCCT kernel — 121 tests, 0 ignored), but the end-to-end path is not wired up. Closing this gap requires: (1) adding realization syntax to bracket.ri, (2) compiler emitting RealizationDecls with geometry ops, (3) implementing the CLI, and (4) an e2e integration test.
tasks.details:
## What's missing

1. **examples/bracket.ri** — doesn't exist. The bracket source in test fixtures has no geometry/realization syntax. Need to define what realization declarations look like in .ri source (e.g. `realize box(width, height, thickness)` or similar) and add them to the bracket example.

2. **Compiler doesn't emit realizations** — `bracket_compiled_module()` fixture builds TopologyTemplates with params, constraints, and let-bindings but zero RealizationDecls. The compiler's `compile()` function needs to parse realization syntax and produce `RealizationDecl`s containing `CompiledGeometryOp`s (Box, Fillet, etc.).

3. **CLI is a stub** — `crates/reify-cli/src/main.rs` just prints "not yet implemented" and exits. Needs `reify check <file>`, `reify build <file> -o <output>`, and `reify eval <file>` subcommands that wire together parse → compile → Engine.

4. **No e2e integration test** — Need a test that: parses bracket source → compiles → creates Engine with real OcctKernel → calls build() → asserts STEP output contains "ISO-10303-21" and is non-empty.

## Existing pieces that work
- Tree-sitter parser: parses .ri source into ParsedModule (8 tests)
- Compiler: produces TopologyTemplates with value cells + constraints (8 tests)
- Eval engine: evaluates values, checks constraints, calls geometry kernel (24 tests)
- OCCT kernel: creates Box/Cylinder/Sphere, booleans, fillet, export STEP (15 tests)
- All 121 tests pass

## Key design question
What does realization syntax look like in .ri? The language spec may define this. The compiler needs to know how to lower realization declarations into CompiledGeometryOp sequences.

tasks.title: PersistentMap abstraction + im-rs integration
tasks.description: Add the `im` crate to the workspace and create a PersistentMap abstraction in reify-types. Migrate ValueMap from std::HashMap to im::HashMap with structural sharing.
tasks.details: Completed. PersistentMap with im::HashMap provides O(1) clone via structural sharing.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test --workspace passes. PersistentMap clone is O(1).

tasks.title: Freshness model + Priority types
tasks.description: Update the Freshness enum to the spec's 4-variant model and add Priority/Task types to reify-runtime for the sequential scheduler.
tasks.details: Completed. Freshness has 4 variants (Final, Intermediate, Pending, Failed). Priority and Task types in reify-runtime.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test --workspace passes. No references to old Freshness variants remain.

tasks.title: Snapshot + EvaluationGraph + node data types
tasks.description: Implement Snapshot with provenance tracking and EvaluationGraph with PersistentMap-backed node storage. Build graph from TopologyTemplate.
tasks.details: Completed. Snapshot and EvaluationGraph with PersistentMap-backed storage, graph construction from TopologyTemplate.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test --workspace passes. Snapshot clone is O(1) via structural sharing.

tasks.title: Dependency tracking + reverse index
tasks.description: Implement Adapton-style dynamic dependency traces during expression evaluation, and a ReverseDependencyIndex for dirty propagation.
tasks.details: Completed. TraceRecorder captures reads during evaluation, ReverseDependencyIndex inverts edges for dirty propagation.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test --workspace passes. Traces capture correct reads, reverse index correctly maps dependents.

tasks.title: Content-hash caching + early cutoff
tasks.description: Implement NodeCache with version fast path and content-hash early cutoff. If a node re-evaluates to the same result, don't propagate dirty to dependents.
tasks.details: Completed. NodeCache with version fast path and content-hash early cutoff prevents unnecessary cascading recomputation.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test --workspace passes. Early cutoff prevents downstream re-evaluation when results unchanged.

tasks.title: Dirty/demand cone + sequential scheduler
tasks.description: Implement DemandRegistry, dirty cone propagation, demand cone computation, dirty∩demand intersection, and a sequential priority-ordered scheduler in reify-runtime.
tasks.details: Completed. DemandRegistry, dirty/demand cone intersection, and sequential scheduler with topological sort.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test --workspace passes. Only dirty∩demand nodes re-evaluated.

tasks.title: Integration: incremental evaluator pipeline
tasks.description: Replace the M1 simple evaluator with the new incremental engine. Wire Snapshot, EvaluationGraph, caching, dirty/demand into Engine. All M1 tests pass plus new incremental behavior tests.
tasks.details: Completed. Engine uses Snapshot + CacheStore + dirty/demand scheduling. edit_param() incrementally re-evaluates only dirty∩demand nodes.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test --workspace passes. All M1 tests pass, incremental re-evaluation verified.

tasks.title: Handle geometry build errors gracefully
tasks.description: Geometry build errors are caught and reported without panicking, allowing the eval pipeline to continue.
tasks.details: Completed. Graceful error handling for geometry build failures in the eval pipeline.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-eval passes with geometry error tests.

tasks.title: Add test for consecutive edit_param() calls
tasks.description: Test that multiple consecutive edit_param() calls work correctly with incremental re-evaluation.
tasks.details: Completed. Test verifies consecutive parameter edits produce correct incremental results.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-eval -- consecutive_edit passes.

tasks.title: Core resolution types in reify-types

tasks.details: Completed. Core resolution types added to reify-types for auto parameter resolution.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-types passes.

tasks.details: Completed. auto keyword in grammar, parser produces AutoParam nodes, compiler emits auto-flagged ValueCells.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-syntax -p reify-compiler passes with auto keyword tests.

tasks.title: Resolution engine — wire solver into eval pipeline

tasks.details: Completed. Resolution engine invokes solver for auto params, integrates with incremental evaluation and caching.

tasks.title: Concurrent evaluate() with async fan-out
tasks.description: Implement concurrent evaluation where independent nodes in the eval graph fan out to parallel async tasks.
tasks.details: Concurrent evaluate() fans out independent nodes to tokio tasks, respecting dependency order and demand.

tasks.title: Event journal

tasks.details: Completed. Event journal records eval events with timestamps, supports replay and filtering.

tasks.title: Commitment policy + priority promotion
tasks.description: Implement commitment policies that control when speculative results are committed, with priority promotion for demanded nodes.
tasks.details: Commitment policy determines when speculative/intermediate results become final. Priority promotion escalates node priority when demanded.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-runtime passes with commitment policy tests.

tasks.title: LSP server — core infrastructure

tasks.details: LSP server using tower-lsp, handles textDocument/didOpen, didChange, diagnostics from eval pipeline.
tasks.testStrategy: cargo test -p reify-lsp passes with LSP protocol tests.

tasks.title: LSP features — hover, go-to-def, completions

tasks.title: M4 integration — interactive edit loop E2E
